name: golang-github-go-playground-universal-translator-dev
title: golang-github-go-playground-universal-translator-dev
summary: translator for Go using CLDR data and pluralization rules
description:
translator for Go using CLDR data and pluralization rules
Universal Translator is an i18n Translator for Go using CLDR data and
pluralization rules. This package is a thin wrapper around its
locales counterpart (golang-github-go-playground-locales-dev) in
order to store and translate text for use in your applications.
.
Features:
- Rules generated from the CLDR data
- Contains Cardinal, Ordinal and Range Plural Rules
- Contains Month, Weekday and Timezone translations
- Contains Date & Time formatting functions
- Contains Number, Currency, Accounting and Percent formatting
functions
- Supports the Gregorian calendar only
- Supports loading translations from files
- Supports exporting translations to files
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-go-playground-universal-translator-dev because translator for Go using CLDR data and pluralization rules.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-go-playground-universal-translator-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-go-playground-universal-translator-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-go-playground-universal-translator-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
